inputs.label: Building Length
inputs.businessContext: Overall length of the building footprint along the ridge direction.

businessContext_i18n.en: Overall length of the building footprint along the ridge direction.

inputs.label: Building Width
inputs.businessContext: Overall width of the building footprint perpendicular to the ridge.

businessContext_i18n.en: Overall width of the building footprint perpendicular to the ridge.

inputs.label: Roof Pitch (rise/run ratio)
inputs.businessContext: Ratio of vertical rise to horizontal run (e.g., 0.5 = 6:12 pitch).

businessContext_i18n.en: Ratio of vertical rise to horizontal run (e.g., 0.5 = 6:12 pitch).

inputs.label: Eave Overhang Length
inputs.businessContext: Horizontal projection of the roof beyond the exterior wall.

businessContext_i18n.en: Horizontal projection of the roof beyond the exterior wall.

inputs.label: Roof Type
inputs.businessContext: Geometric configuration of the roof structure.

businessContext_i18n.en: Geometric configuration of the roof structure.

inputs.label: Number of Floors
inputs.businessContext: Number of stories; affects complexity and potential waste factors.

businessContext_i18n.en: Number of stories; affects complexity and potential waste factors.

inputs.label: Material Waste Factor
inputs.businessContext: Percentage allowance for cutting, overlap, and installation losses (ISO 9001 waste reduction).

businessContext_i18n.en: Percentage allowance for cutting, overlap, and installation losses (ISO 9001 waste reduction).

rules.message: Roof pitch must be between 0.1 and 2.0 (rise/run).
Overhang length exceeds 25% of building width; verify design.
Waste factor above 20% triggers Lean review (Six Sigma DMAIC).
Hip roofs require minimum pitch of 0.25 for proper drainage.

thresholds.message: Roof area exceeds 2000 m²; consider structural reinforcement review.
Waste factor >15% indicates potential process inefficiency (Lean Kaizen opportunity).
Steep pitch (rise/run >1.0) requires safety harness protocols (OSHA compliance).

formulas.description: Horizontal footprint area of the building including overhang.
Slope multiplier based on roof pitch (rise/run).
Total roof surface area before waste and type adjustments.
Multiplier for roof type complexity (gable=1.0, hip=1.15, flat=1.0, shed=1.05).
formulas.formula: CASE roof_type WHEN 'gable' THEN 1.0 WHEN 'hip' THEN 1.15 WHEN 'flat' THEN 1.0 WHEN 'shed' THEN 1.05 END

formulas.description: Roof area adjusted for type complexity.
Final material area including waste factor.
Primary calculable roof area (net usable area for material estimation).

primary.label: Net Roof Area
primary.description: Primary result: total roof surface area adjusted for type and pitch, excluding waste.

hiddenLossDrivers.label: Complexity Loss (type factor >1.0)
hiddenLossDrivers.description: Additional area due to roof type complexity beyond gable baseline.

hiddenLossDrivers.label: Waste Loss
hiddenLossDrivers.description: Material area lost due to cutting, overlap, and installation waste.

hiddenLossDrivers.label: Pitch Penalty
hiddenLossDrivers.description: Extra area from slope compared to flat roof (pitch factor >1.0).

suggestedActions.action: Implement Lean 5S and standardized work to reduce waste factor below 10%.
Consider reducing pitch to 0.5-0.75 to lower material cost and improve safety.
Evaluate if a simpler roof type (gable) can meet functional requirements, reducing area by up to 15%.
Reduce overhang to 0.6 m standard to cut material and wind load.
